title: Isotopic Labeling of Cyanobacteria and DNA Analysis
author: Bonnie Poulos
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.c9nz5d
materials: Growth medium (Pro99, SN, SNAX or Amp1)

steps.title: Prepare 15N Labeled Medium
steps.instruction: Prepare Pro99, SN, SNAX or Amp1 medium according to directions, but use 15N ammonium chloride which will provide the heavy isotope. 15NH4Cl is available from Cambridge Isotope Laboratories, Inc. (#NLM-467-1). AMMONIUM CHLORIDE (15N, 99%) Cambridge Isotope Laboratories, Inc. Catalog #NLM-467-1

steps.title: Grow Cyanobacteria in Heavy Nitrogen
steps.instruction: Grow cyanobacteria in the medium with heavy nitrogen and transfer at least 3 times before use.

steps.title: Harvest Bacteria
steps.instruction: Harvest the bacteria grown in heavy nitrogen.

steps.title: Extract Bacterial DNA
steps.instruction: Extract bacterial DNA using standard methods.

steps.title: Quantify DNA
steps.instruction: Quantify the DNA using Quant-iT Pico Green. Quant-it™ PicoGreen® dsDNA Assay Kit Life Technologies Catalog #P7589

steps.title: Prepare DNA for Centrifugation
steps.instruction: Use at least 10 µg of DNA for density gradient centrifugation.

steps.title: Prepare Centrifugation Equipment
steps.instruction: For density gradient centrifugation, a Beckman VTi 65 vertical rotor was used with 13x48 mm OptiSeal polyallomer tubes (4.9 ml capacity).

steps.title: Mix DNA with TE Buffer
steps.instruction: Mix the DNA with TE buffer (10mM Tris, 1mM EDTA, pH7.6) to a final volume of 0.9 ml.

steps.title: Prepare CsCl-DNA Solution
steps.instruction: Mix the DNA with 4ml of CsCl prepared in TE to a density of p1.8 (measure the density of the final solutions, they should be p1.7).

steps.title: Dispense Solution into Tubes
steps.instruction: Dispense 4.9 ml of the DNA sample in CsCl into the OptiSeal tube and plug with the black caps.

steps.title: Load Tubes into Rotor
steps.instruction: Load the tubes into the rotor and put caps on all carriers.

steps.title: Perform Ultracentrifugation
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 44,000 rpm (=184,678.5 g) in a Beckman L70 or L80 ultracentrifuge for 48 hr at 18°C. 48:00:00 Note: Do not centrifuge at lower temperatures as CsCl may precipitate out.

steps.title: Quantify DNA in Fractions
steps.instruction: Calculate amount of DNA in each fraction using Quant-iT Pico Green (perform in duplicate) and measure the density of each fraction.

steps.title: Determine DNA Density
steps.instruction: Determine the density of the fractions with DNA (plot ng of DNA versus CsCl density).
